message.content:
<deliberation>
Task: Evaluate each main GDPR node (REG-092, REG-035, REG-095, REG-021, REG-017) against the provided policy chunk, integrating any referenced nodes where the main node cross-references them. Identify deviations only where there is a difference in constraints/scope, not mere omissions. Provide exact GDPR and policy quotes for any deviation. Classify deviations per taxonomy.

Policy chunk key points:
- States a right to object when processing is based on Art 6(1)(f): “you may object ... at any time.” No mention of grounds “relating to particular situation,” no mention of 6(1)(e), nor profiling or balancing test (these are omissions).
- States a right to lodge a complaint “if you believe ... infringes data protection law,” citing Art 77 GDPR (broader than “this Regulation”).
- Contact details and a “Provision of data” note (not central to these nodes).
  
Node-by-node:

REG-092 (Art 77(1)):
- GDPR requires complaint right “if ... infringes this Regulation,” and indicates Member State locations where to lodge (omission if not present).
- Policy states complaint right if infringing “data protection law,” which broadens trigger beyond “this Regulation.” That is over-compliance (severity deviation). Quote both.
- No need for cross-reference for 77(1); 77(1) does not itself cite another article. Omissions (locations, “without prejudice”) are Non-Deviation.

REG-035 (Art 21(1)):
- GDPR: right to object “on grounds relating to his or her particular situation,” at any time, to processing based on 6(1)(e) or (f), including profiling; controller may continue if compelling legitimate grounds override or for legal claims.
- Policy: “If ... based on Art 6(1)(f) ... you may object ... at any time.” It omits the particular-situation condition; this unconditional wording is broader than GDPR—over-compliance (severity deviation). Provide GDPR quote including the “on grounds...” clause and policy quote.
- Not treating non-mention of 6(1)(e), profiling, or balancing test as deviations (omissions).

Cross-reference: includes Art 6(1) (REG-001), as Art 21(1) cites it.

REG-095 (Art 78(2)):
- GDPR: right to effective judicial remedy if the competent SA doesn’t handle a complaint or doesn’t inform within three months; references Arts 55, 56, and 77.
- Policy chunk does not address judicial remedy or three-month rule—omission, not deviation. No contradictory statements.
- Considered references: Art 77(1) (REG-092) and Art 77(2) (REG-093) for context on the complaint process linkage.

REG-021 (Art 15(1)):
- GDPR: access right and enumerated information (a)-(h), referencing Art 22(1) and (4) for ADM/profiling, and right to lodge complaint (Art 77).
- Policy chunk does not provide specific Art 15 content—omission. It does mention complaint right generally. No contradictions.
- Considered references: Art 22(1) (REG-041) and Art 77(1) (REG-092).

REG-017 (Art 14(2)):
- GDPR: additional info when data not obtained from the data subject; references 14(1), 6(1)(f), 9(2)(a), 22(1) and (4).
- Policy chunk does not provide these details (storage period, legitimate interests, rights incl. portability, withdraw consent, source, ADM). Omissions, not deviations.
- Considered references: 14(1) (REG-016), 6(1) (REG-001), 9(2) (REG-009), 22(1) (REG-041).

Proceed to construct JSON with deviations for REG-092 (severity) and REG-035 (severity); others marked as no deviations.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.",
        "policy_quote": "In accordance with Art 77 GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.",
        "mismatch": "Policy expands the complaint trigger from infringement of “this Regulation” to any “data protection law,” over-broadening the basis for complaint.",
        "analysis": "GDPR ties the complaint right in Art 77(1) specifically to infringements of the GDPR, whereas the policy grants the right for broader ‘data protection law’ infringements, which is more permissive toward data subjects."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-035",
    "clause": "Art21(1)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-001"
    ],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, at any time to processing of personal data concerning him or her which is based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1)",
        "policy_quote": "Right to object to processing: If the processing of your data is based on Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR, you may object to the processing at any time.",
        "mismatch": "Policy allows unconditional objection to processing based on Art 6(1)(f), whereas GDPR requires that the objection be based on grounds relating to the data subject’s particular situation.",
        "analysis": "By omitting the ‘particular situation’ condition, the policy broadens the right to object beyond the GDPR’s requirement. Non-mention of Art 6(1)(e), profiling, or the balancing exception are treated as omissions, not deviations."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-095",
    "clause": "Art78(2)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-092",
      "REG-093"
    ],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-041",
      "REG-092"
    ],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": [
      "REG-016",
      "REG-001",
      "REG-009",
      "REG-041"
    ],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
